Error rendering equation: '\\langle\\zeta_i(t)\\zeta_j(t')\\right)\\rangle = \\left\\langle K_{im}K_{jm}e^{i(\\theta_m(t)-\\theta_j(t))}\\right)\\rangle = \\sum_{m,n}\\left(\\frac{k^2}{N}\\delta_{mn} + \\frac{K^2}{N^2}\\right)e^{i(\\theta_m(t)-\\theta_n(t))}. \\ (10)

Assuming uncorrelated oscillators yields

\\left\\langle e^{i(\\theta_m(t)-\\theta_n(t))}\\right)\\rangle = \\delta_{mn} \\left\\langle e^{i(\\theta_m(t)-\\theta_m(t))}\\right), \\ (11)

leading to the simplified form

\\left\\langle\\zeta_i(t)\\zeta_i(t')\\right) = (k^2 + K^2/N)\\left\\langle e^{i(\\theta_i(t)-\\theta_i(t))}\\right). \\ (12)

The right-hand side thus represents the autocorrelation of a single oscillator's phase pointer. In the Fourier domain, the network noise spectrum S_\\zeta(\\omega) is proportional to the power spectrum of a single oscillator averaged over the network:

S_\\zeta(\\omega) = (k^2 + K^2/N)S_\\zeta(\\omega), \\ (13)
